blockers.description: The question asks for patients “under 30 years old” who are also “older patients,” which is contradictory.
example_questions: What does "older patient" mean when patients are also specified to be under 30?
How should I interprete old patients under 30 years old?
What criteria should be used to classify a patient as older?
blockers.resolution: Use an “older patient” as a patient whose time in the system is ≥ 180 days, measured as the difference between Laboratory.Date and Patient.[First Date].

blockers.description: The term "core monitoring program" is referenced in the question but is not defined in the provided business information or database descriptions.
example_questions: What qualifies a patient as being enrolled in the core monitoring program?
What conditions indicate that a patient is participating in the core monitoring program?
How can enrollment in the core monitoring program be identified?
blockers.resolution: Patients enrolled in the core monitoring program are defined as those who have more than one laboratory record.

blockers.description: The definition of "standard evaluation period" is provided but does not specify an exact timeframe that can be directly applied to the data.
example_questions: What exact date range defines the standard evaluation period?
Which laboratory record dates should be considered part of the standard evaluation period?
Is the standard evaluation period tied to a specific year or a set of dates?
blockers.resolution: The standard evaluation period is defined as laboratory records dated within the calendar year 1994.
